disruptions.title: South Western Railway – Guildford line delays
disruptions.detail: Signalling fault between Woking and Guildford. Trains running 25–40 minutes late. Replacement buses operating from Woking.
disruptions.staff_impact: 2 team members commuting from Woking may be late. Check with Jade (EMP003) and Tariq (EMP007).
disruptions.customer_impact: Commuters diverted through Guildford Bus Station – expect above-normal morning footfall.

disruptions.title: A3 Southbound – lane closure near Guildford
disruptions.detail: Emergency roadworks causing 15-minute delays on the A3 southbound between J10 M25 and Guildford. Expect increased town centre footfall as drivers seek alternatives.
disruptions.customer_impact: Slight increase in lunchtime walk-in trade from office workers avoiding roads.

GLD001.impact_summary: Rail disruption may delay 2 staff members. Expect higher morning footfall from stranded commuters. Staff up the counter from 7:30am.
recommended_actions: Contact Jade and Tariq to confirm their ETA
Brief Sam (shift lead) to cover counter solo until 8:15 if needed
Prepare extra takeaway cups and lid stocks for higher-than-usual grab-and-go demand

disruptions.title: South Western Railway – Guildford line delays
disruptions.detail: Signalling fault between Woking and Guildford. Trains running 25–40 minutes late. Significant passenger build-up on concourse.
disruptions.staff_impact: Jade (EMP003) and Tariq (EMP007) may be significantly late. Call ahead.
disruptions.customer_impact: CRITICAL – Hundreds of stranded commuters on concourse. Expect very long queues from 7:30am onwards. All hands on deck.

GLD002.impact_summary: Station store will experience exceptional demand due to stranded commuters. All available staff must be on counter. Consider calling in off-duty staff if possible.
recommended_actions: Call James Okafor (store manager) immediately to assess staffing options
Pull all available stock from back room to front
Activate queue management – open both till points
Prioritise takeaway drinks over dine-in to maximise throughput

disruptions.title: Great Western Railway – reduced service
disruptions.detail: Reduced timetable due to industrial action. Trains running every 30 minutes instead of every 15 minutes on the Reading–London corridor.
disruptions.staff_impact: No staff impact expected for this store.
disruptions.customer_impact: Fewer commuter-time customers. Oracle shoppers are walk-in and largely unaffected.

disruptions.title: Reading FC Home Match – pre-match traffic
disruptions.detail: Reading FC vs Coventry City kicks off at 15:00. Town centre experiencing above-normal road and pedestrian traffic from 12:00.
disruptions.customer_impact: Pre-match crowd will boost Oracle footfall 12:00–14:00. Expect second surge post-match ~17:00.

RDG001.impact_summary: Rail strike reduces commuter trade but football match more than compensates. Pre-match crowd expected 12–14:00 and post-match 17–18:00.
recommended_actions: Ensure savoury menu items and meal deals are fully stocked for pre-match lunch rush
Brief team on expected footfall peaks
Make sure Costa app loyalty promotions are visible at POS

disruptions.title: Great Western Railway – reduced service
disruptions.detail: Reduced timetable due to industrial action. Trains running every 30 minutes instead of every 15 minutes.
disruptions.customer_impact: Reduced throughput of commuters. Expect queues to be longer but volume lower than typical. Longer dwell times on platform – opportunity for upselling loyalty drinks.

RDG002.impact_summary: Strike reduces commuter volume but longer wait times may increase average spend. Push loyalty app sign-ups and meal deals.
recommended_actions: Brief baristas on loyalty app upsell opportunity
Ensure meal deal signage is prominent
Adjust staffing to match lower-than-usual peak volumes

BAS001.impact_summary: No travel disruptions in the Basingstoke area today. Normal operations expected.
